element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Escort mechanics become core strategy in later rounds
20 recruitable warriors with unique weapons; weapon selection becomes tactical
Consistent across all rounds; escalates with map complexity
Each round culminates in boss fight; difficulty increases per round
Visual theming escalates: Ancient → Medieval → Future → Modern (Genesis exclusive)
Maps grow more intricate; requires route planning and tactical positioning
10 new levels added to Genesis; contemporary setting with escalated challenges

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching exit point with at least one character
Rescuing captives and escorting to safety
Completing stage before time limit expires
typical_tension_triggers: Boss stage (10th stage of each round)
Loss of last active character in party
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Four-round structure with escalating difficulty. Each round comprises 10 stages, culminating in boss encounter. Tension builds through stage progression within round, peaks at stage 10 boss fight, then resets with new thematic epoch. Genesis version extends with Modern Epoch round, maintaining late-game pressure.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Captive characters scattered across levels signal optional rescue paths; rescuing all captives enables character depth but is not strictly required for exit completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Round 1 (Ancient Epoch) introduces core mechanics: movement, basic combat, captive rescue, character switching, and time limits. Starting three-character party and ample enemy spacing allow player adaptation.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Rounds 2-3 (Medieval and Future Epochs) increase enemy density, map complexity, and boss difficulty. Tactical weapon selection and multi-character positioning become essential. Players must choose between speed-running to exit versus thorough enemy/captive clearing.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Round 4 (Future Epoch) and Round 5/Modern Epoch (Genesis exclusive) feature dense enemy concentrations, intricate spatial designs, and time pressure that demands mastery of all mechanics. Boss encounters present significant skill gates.
notable_spikes: Round 4, Stage 10 (Future Epoch boss, culminating original arcade experience)
Round 5 introduction (Modern Epoch on Genesis; 10 new levels with contemporary theme escalating from four-round foundation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized into four rounds, each representing distinct historical/futuristic period: Ancient, Medieval, Future, Modern (Genesis). Each epoch contains 10 stages with consistent thematic aesthetics and progressive mechanical escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Round 1 (Ancient Epoch) vs. Round 5 (Modern Epoch) contrast foundational design with advanced challenges

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage 10 of each round features boss encounter, acting as difficulty checkpoint and narrative milestone. Difficulty increases per round.

Each level can be completed via two routes: (1) guide at least one character to exit, or (2) destroy all enemies. Players choose risk/reward: speed-run exit vs. thorough clearing. Time limit pressures both approaches.

Captive characters scattered throughout levels add complexity: rescuing requires walking over them, then escorting to exit. Death mechanics convert active characters to captives, creating resource management tension.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels; Normal mode emphasizes this; Hard mode removes captives but starts with full 20-character roster

notable_level_patterns.description: 20 recruitable warriors with different weapons create tactical switching opportunities. Choosing weapon loadout for specific level challenges encourages strategic depth.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Consistent across all rounds; complexity increases as players must optimize character selection per stage
